heading: Trilateral scientific and technological cooperation by Egypt, Israel, and United States
label: 3406 Trilateral scientific and technological cooperation by Egypt, Israel, and United States
notes: Amendments1981—Subsec. (c). struck out subsec. (c) which required Presidential report to Congress no later than twelve months after , respecting trilateral cooperative projects between Egypt, Israel, and the United States.Pub. L. 97–113July 20, 1979
amendments: Amendments1981—Subsec. (c). struck out subsec. (c) which required Presidential report to Congress no later than twelve months after , respecting trilateral cooperative projects between Egypt, Israel, and the United States.Pub. L. 97–113July 20, 1979

children.heading: Preparation for United States participation
children.label: a Preparation for United States participation
children.text: It is the sense of the Congress that, in order to continue to build the structure of peace in the Middle East, the United States should be prepared to participate, at an appropriate time, in trilateral cooperative projects of a scientific and technological nature involving Egypt, Israel, and the United States.

potential projects in a variety of areas appropriate for scientific and technological cooperation by the three countries, including agriculture, health, energy, the environment, education, and water resources;
the resources which are available or which would be needed to implement such projects; and
the means by which such projects would be implemented.
